summary: In this very brief post, Joel Spolsky explains the decision to give each Stack Exchange site its own unique domain name and brand identity rather than using generic Stack Exchange subdomains. He argues that individually-branded sites feel more authentic and trustworthy to their communities. The post links out to a longer explanation on the Stack Overflow blog.
key_points: Individual branding for community sites creates a stronger sense of identity and belonging than generic numbering schemes
Authenticity and trust are enhanced when sites have their own domain names and visual identities
Community coherence matters more than corporate brand consistency when building network sites
money_quotes: We decided that individually-branded sites felt more authentic and trustworthy.
Nobody wants to say that they live in Housing Block 2938TC. They want to live in Colonial Manor.
key_insight: Giving community sites their own unique brand identities rather than generic labels fosters authenticity, trust, and a stronger sense of belonging among users.
